items.summary: Long-standing, documented site-to-site VPN tunnel from edge-rtr01 to a partner network (Meridian Exchange Carrier, 198.51.100.20).
items.correct_label: Benign — a long-standing, documented site-to-site VPN tunnel to a partner network (Meridian Exchange Carrier, 198.51.100.20); not part of any intrusion.
items.decisive_evidence_description: The tunnel's presence in edge-rtr01's config diff as unchanged/expected, its age (predates the incident window by over a year), and the OOB tap's confirmation that it is the only persistent external tunnel observed. Being a GRE/VPN tunnel to an external IP is NOT by itself grounds for suspicion.

items.summary: Documented monitoring SPAN session from core-rtr01 to NOC-MON01, change ticket CHG-2026-0731, requested by svc-noc-mon.
items.correct_label: Benign — a documented monitoring SPAN session from core-rtr01 to NOC-MON01 (change ticket CHG-2026-0731, requested by svc-noc-mon); not part of any intrusion.
items.decisive_evidence_description: The change ticket CHG-2026-0731 in the GRC system of record, the requesting service account svc-noc-mon, and the SPAN session's long-running, unremarkable history. A SPAN/mirror session existing on a core device is not, by itself, suspicious — it is ordinary infrastructure here.

items.summary: An undocumented but genuine config change to fw01 by neng-tpatel on 2026-06-03 (NAT/ACL adjustment for a vendor integration), made with no change ticket.
items.correct_label: Benign — a genuine, authorized configuration change to fw01 by administrator neng-tpatel on 2026-06-03 that was simply never ticketed; not part of any intrusion.
items.decisive_evidence_description: AAA records the session as neng-tpatel from NOC-JUMP01 (10.20.10.10), an established admin account on its usual jump host, and the change itself (a NAT/ACL adjustment for a vendor integration) has no connection to edge-rtr01 or any external IP of concern. The absence of a change ticket is NOT, by itself, evidence of compromise — undocumented-but-legitimate admin work occurs routinely in this environment.

items.summary: 2026-06-05 to 06-07 activity by contractor account extpt-coalridge against edge-rtr01 and fw01, including a temporary GRE test tunnel to 192.0.2.50, torn down at engagement close.
items.correct_label: Authorized — a pre-approved penetration test (engagement PT-2026-04, 2026-06-05 to 06-07, scoped to edge-rtr01 and fw01); not part of any intrusion. Its temporary test tunnel (to 192.0.2.50) was torn down at engagement close, consistent with the OOB tap observing it only during the engagement window.
items.decisive_evidence_description: The PT-2026-04 engagement record in the GRC system of record (vendor Coalridge Security, authorized window 2026-06-05 to 06-07, scope edge-rtr01+fw01, approved source IPs 192.0.2.10-12, contractor account extpt-coalridge, status Completed). The contractor account's activity, source IPs, or the existence of its temporary test tunnel are NOT sufficient on their own to authorize or implicate it — the engagement record is what confirms authorization and confirms the test tunnel was torn down at close.

items.summary: 2026-06-08 credential-spray burst (~500 attempts) from 103.41.0.0/24 against edge-rtr01 and fw01, zero successes.
items.correct_label: Unrelated noise — a failed credential-spray burst from 103.41.0.0/24 against edge-rtr01 and fw01 on 2026-06-08 with zero successful authentications; not evidence of any compromise.
items.decisive_evidence_description: Explicit confirmation that zero authentications from 103.41.0.0/24 succeeded against edge-rtr01 or fw01, at any time. The attempt volume (~500) or the fact that it targeted these devices is NOT sufficient on its own to implicate this burst — only the zero-successful-authentications check rules it out.
